title: Static market calendars quietly break AI trading bots in India
author: TheOldSoul15
selftext:
A lot of AI trading stacks focus on signal generation and ignore the timing layer until something breaks live.

In Indian markets that usually means:
- holiday logic drifting year to year
- MCX and equity sessions behaving differently
- bots validating trades against stale market hours
- execution layers assuming the market is open when it isn’t

That failure mode is boring, but it is real.

We turned that support layer into a small MIT Python package so the calendar and trading-hours logic can sit separately from the model layer.

Use case:
- LLM or ML model generates a signal
- calendar layer validates whether the target market is actually tradable
- execution engine decides whether to route or block the order

PyPI:
https://pypi.org/project/aion-indian-market-calendar/

Not posting this as a brand pitch. It is support infrastructure for anyone building AI or bot-driven trading systems around Indian markets.

If you already solved this differently, would be useful to know how you are handling session-state validation in production.
